Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1182 upvotes, 163 comments
  2. Defeat as Method 34 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Exploring the ocean with Raspberry Pi–powered marine robots 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 215 upvotes, 74 comments
  5. Converting Binary Floating-Point Numbers to Shortest Decimal Strings 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. HyperCard discovery: Neuromancer, Count Zero, Mona Lisa Overdrive (2022) 55 upvotes, 15 comments
  7. Open Weights isn't Open Training 59 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 196 upvotes, 74 comments
  9. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to “The Office” (2009) 268 upvotes, 116 comments
  10. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 26 upvotes, 6 comments
  11. I put my whole life into a single database 382 upvotes, 185 comments
  12. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 150 upvotes, 67 comments
  13. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 56 upvotes, 21 comments
  14. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 56 upvotes, 22 comments
  15. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 451 upvotes, 268 comments
  16. Levels of Agentic Engineering 63 upvotes, 30 comments
  17. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 56 upvotes, 26 comments
  18. Meta acquires Moltbook 320 upvotes, 213 comments
  19. Billion-Parameter Theories 78 upvotes, 44 comments
  20. Rebasing in Magit 162 upvotes, 112 comments
  21. Agents that run while I sleep 124 upvotes, 86 comments
  22. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 226 upvotes, 180 comments
  23. RISC-V Is Sloooow 64 upvotes, 49 comments
  24. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 343 upvotes, 344 comments
  25. Iowa Payphone Defends Itself (Associated Press, 1984) 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 29 upvotes, 22 comments
  27. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 44 upvotes, 45 comments
  28. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 204 upvotes, 279 comments
  29. I built a programming language using Claude Code 82 upvotes, 121 comments
  30. After outages, Amazon to make senior engineers sign off on AI-assisted changes 136 upvotes, 237 comments

Last generated 5:30am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
